history.title: History
history.intro: A story of faith, neighborhood and commitment passed down through generations.
history.section1.title: Our origins
history.section1.text: The brotherhood was founded with a vocation for worship, formation and charity, rooted in its parish and people.
history.section2.title: Heritage and devotion
history.section2.text: Over the years, the corporation has preserved an artistic and spiritual legacy that remains alive today.
history.section3.title: Present and future
history.section3.text: Today we continue with the same purpose: serving the Church, the neighborhood and our titular images.
titulares.title: Titular Images and Heritage
titulares.subtitle: Contemplation, beauty and faith in every detail.
contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: We are at your disposal for any inquiry.
contact.name: Name
contact.subject: Subject
contact.successText: Your message has been sent, may our Titular Images guide you.
home.quick.member.title: Become a member
home.quick.member.text: Apply for membership with a short guided form.
home.quick.docs.title: Documentation
home.quick.docs.text: Download statutes, rules and official documents by category.
home.quick.shop.title: Souvenir shop
member.title: Become a member
member.subtitle: Fill in your details to start your membership request. The secretariat will contact you.
member.successText: Thank you. We have received your request; the brotherhood will contact you.
documentation.title: Documentation
documentation.subtitle: Files available for members and visitors.
shop.title: Souvenir shop
shop.subtitle: A space for keepsakes and brotherhood items.
shop.comingSoon: The online shop will be available soon.
home.donation.title: Donation for the Lord's float gilding project
home.donation.lead: We are raising funds for the restoration and gilding of Our Lord's processional float. Every contribution, large or small, supports our devotion and becomes part of the brotherhood's story.
home.donation.note: You can request details of the dedicated account, project timeline and donation receipts from the office or via the contact form.

home.chapel.weekdaysChapel: Chapel: 10 a.m. to 1:30 p.m. and 6 p.m. to 9 p.m.
home.chapel.adoration: Eucharistic adoration, Thursdays, 6 p.m. to 7:50 p.m.
